prompt.content:
My CHRO has a board meeting today and our CEO just told her that she needs to come prepared with metrics as they have a concern whether we are giving our internal candidates a fair shot before going external and are curious if our external candidates are worthy of the added spend to get higher quality candidates. She needs me to prep a quick analysis and I need your help. Grab the exec comparison matrix, calculate the percentage difference in average Overall Score between the two source types and identify which group scores higher.

Do not round intermediary calculations. Provide your final numerical response as a percentage rounded to one decimal place. Please provide your response as a message in the console.

rubrics.name: States that based on the Executive Comparison Matrix, the candidate source... (ver_a229250d223c4f4088d8c95bd6cd5974)

messages.content: States that based on the Executive Comparison Matrix, the candidate source type that has the highest average Overall Score is Internal Candidates.

rubrics.name: States that based on the Executive Comparison Matrix, the percentage... (ver_dd8ac738276c4a579a44555a8ea1ff69)

messages.content: States that based on the Executive Comparison Matrix, the percentage difference between the internal and external candidates is 3.8% (acceptable range 3.76% to 3.84%).

golden_responses.golden_text_response: Based on the Executive Comparison Matrix, the candidate source type that has the highest average Overall Score is Internal Candidates. The percentage difference between the internal and external candidates is 3.8%.
